content_markdown: A satellite X of mass $m$ orbits the Earth with a period $T$. What will be the orbital period of satellite Y of mass $2m$ occupying the same orbit as X?
vibe_explanation: The orbital period of a satellite depends only on the mass of the central body (Earth) and the radius of the orbit, as shown by Kepler's Third Law or by equating gravitational force to centripetal force ($G\frac{Mm}{r^2} = m\frac{4\pi^2r}{T^2}$). The mass of the satellite $m$ cancels out, meaning the period is independent of the satellite's mass.
